assertion_results.text: Validation identifies that the Bug description is missing the Steps to Reproduce section (required by bug-template-mock.md) (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: validation-result.md line 40: "Steps to Reproduce (`### **Steps to Reproduce**`): MISSING -- no heading matching this format exists in the bug description." Also listed in the validation outcome on line 46: "Bug ACME-501 is missing required sections: Steps to Reproduce, Expected Result, Environment / Version."

assertion_results.text: Validation identifies that the Bug description is missing the Expected Result section (required by bug-template-mock.md) (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: validation-result.md line 41: "Expected Result (`### **Expected Result**`): MISSING -- no heading matching this format exists in the bug description." Also listed in the validation outcome on line 46: "Bug ACME-501 is missing required sections: Steps to Reproduce, Expected Result, Environment / Version."

assertion_results.text: The skill stops execution after identifying missing sections — it does NOT proceed to Steps 2-5 (Step 1 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: validation-result.md line 48: "**Execution stopped at Step 1.** Per the triage-bug skill specification, when any required section is missing from the bug description, the skill must stop immediately and not proceed to Steps 2-5 (Reproduce/Trace, Codebase Investigation, Root Cause Analysis, Affects Version Resolution, or Task Generation)." The output file contains only Step 0 and Step 1 sections — no Steps 2-5 appear.

assertion_results.text: The error message lists the specific missing sections and references the Bug template path from Bug Configuration
assertion_results.evidence: validation-result.md line 46: "Bug ACME-501 is missing required sections: Steps to Reproduce, Expected Result, Environment / Version. The bug description does not follow the template at docs/templates/bug-template.md." This lists each missing section by name and references the configured Bug template path.
